title: Build a worker that survives every known iron-law failure mode
prompt: Write `MyApp.Workers.OrderFulfillmentWorker` — a production-grade worker that must defeat every iron-law trap: (1) Non-idempotent: store `fulfilled_at` in the Order record and short-circuit on retry. (2) Atom keys in args: use string keys in both enqueue helper AND perform/1 destructuring. (3) Storing structs: do NOT pass `%Order{}` or `%User{}` in args — pass `%{"order_id" => order.id}` and load inside perform/1. (4) Also handle: rate-limited payment gateway with `{:snooze, retry_after}`, permanent failures with `{:cancel, reason}`, transient errors with `{:error, reason}`, proper `backoff/1` override that uses `unsaved_error` to detect rate-limit errors, and a unique constraint `[period: 3600, keys: [:order_id], states: [:available, :scheduled, :executing, :retryable, :completed, :cancelled, :discarded]]`. perform/1 must use an `Ecto.Multi` to atomically update `fulfilled_at` and write the fulfillment record. Add a `@moduledoc` that enumerates each iron law and how this worker defends against it. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/order_fulfillment_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: The ultimate integration check — every identified failure mode in the research dossier must be absent AND every correct pattern must be present. Designed so any one omission drops the score below 0.9.
research_citation: oliver-kriska/claude-elixir-phoenix Iron Laws: 'Jobs must be idempotent. Args use string keys. Never store structs in args.'
